message:
chore(beads): close ntm-952.2.1 (CLI error format standardization)

Marks the CLI error format standardization task as complete.
Implementation adds CLIError struct with remediation hints and
pre-defined error factories for common scenarios.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-951","title":"Bug: Harden SSH remote tmux arg quoting","description":"Remote tmux execution uses: ssh host tmux <args...>. OpenSSH builds a single remote command string, so args with spaces/metacharacters can break and enable remote-shell injection. Fix by constructing a single remote command with strict shell-escaping (tmux + each arg), add unit tests, and ensure all remote tmux calls are safe/reliable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-12T11:34:14.088049176-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T11:40:41.389206298-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-12T11:40:41.389206298-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-951","depends_on_id":"ntm-5zs","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
-{"id":"ntm-952","title":"EPIC: Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)","description":"# Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)

## Goal
Make NTM feel calm, obvious, and premium — like a best-in-class product — while staying true to terminal constraints.

This is a **polish program epic**: it coordinates UX improvements across CLI + TUI, emphasizing:
- Clear information hierarchy (less visual noise)
- Predictable navigation (stable keymaps + help overlays)
- Actionable copy (every error tells you what to do next)
- Smoothness (minimal flicker/jitter, fast refresh, responsive interactions)
- Accessibility (reduced motion, icon fallbacks, contrast)

## Target User Journeys
1. **First run**: install → `ntm deps` → `ntm tutorial` → `ntm spawn` → `ntm palette` → `ntm dashboard`
2. **Daily loop**: attach → palette → send → dashboard → copy/save → detach
3. **Remote over SSH**: sessions persist, minimal surprises, no brittle quoting, clear "where am I" context

## Scope
- CLI: copywriting, error remediation, progress affordances
- Palette: search + target selection clarity + recents/favorites
- Dashboard: stable layout + help/legend + better empty/loading/error states
- Theme/accessibility: reduced motion + safe icon fallbacks + contrast
- Performance: refresh budgets, caching, graceful degradation
- Docs alignment (update existing docs; avoid new doc sprawl)

## Out of Scope (tracked elsewhere)
- New major product surfaces (e.g., VS Code extension) → see `ntm-97c5`
- New data sources/panels already covered under dashboard program → see `ntm-jpp1`
- Large config discovery work → see `ntm-bke.1`

## Related / Dependencies (foundations)
- Dashboard foundation: `ntm-jpp1`, `ntm-im5l`, `ntm-p99w`, `ntm-amou`
- Width + layout primitives: `ntm-ci35`
- Refresh cadence: `ntm-0c8q`
- Theme system: `ntm-ed5`, `ntm-ube`
- Context/session inference: `ntm-4ji`, `ntm-bke.1`","design":"## Design Principles (Stripe-level in terminal)
- **Clarity over cleverness**: effects exist to support hierarchy, not compete with content.
- **Predictability**: stable keymaps; no surprise mode switches; consistent back/escape behavior.
- **Actionability**: errors include cause + fix + suggested next command.
- **Continuity**: preserve scroll position / selection where possible; avoid jitter.
- **Graceful fallback**: icons, colors, widths, and animations degrade cleanly.","acceptance_criteria":"- A consistent help/legend affordance exists in palette + dashboard.
- CLI errors are standardized and always include a remediation suggestion.
- Reduced-motion mode exists and disables pulsing/shimmer animations.
- Dashboard/palette interactions feel stable (minimal flicker/jump under refresh).
- Docs reflect the actual UX (keys, tiers, environment variables).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00"}
-{"id":"ntm-952.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: UX Design System & Consistency","description":"Focus: consistency and “calm premium” feel across all TUIs.

Deliverables:
- Shared layout primitives and style tokens (spacing, borders, typography)
- Shared interaction patterns (help overlay, key hints, empty/loading states)
- A small set of conventions used everywhere (no per-screen one-offs)

Non-goals:
- Rewriting all screens at once
- Adding new major product features","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:
- Standard error format (cause → fix → next command)
- Better session selection/resolution when args omitted
- Consistent “what next” guidance after major commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
-{"id":"ntm-952.3","title":"SUB-EPIC: Command Palette Premium","description":"Focus: command palette that feels fast, obvious, and delightful.

Deliverables:
- Recents/favorites/pinning
- Crystal-clear target selection (who will receive this?)
- Preview that reduces misfires (metadata, warnings)
- Help overlay and consistent navigation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.793248034-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.793248034-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
-{"id":"ntm-952.4","title":"SUB-EPIC: Dashboard Premium","description":"Focus: dashboard interactions, information hierarchy, and stability under refresh.

Deliverables:
- Help/legend overlay and consistent keymap
- Better empty/loading/error states with retry affordances
- Reduced layout jitter/flicker
- Quick actions that match the real workflow","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.819145086-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.819145086-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:
- Reduced-motion mode (no shimmer/pulse)
- Safer icon and width fallbacks (no broken gutters)
- Better contrast defaults and an escape hatch for low-color terminals","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

- Refresh budgets and caching (avoid expensive tmux capture loops)
- Graceful degradation when tmux data is missing/slow
- Debug affordances for diagnosing “why is this slow?”","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:
- Update existing docs (README, command_palette.md) to match UX + keys
- Add workflow cookbook sections in existing docs (avoid new doc sprawl)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.898073661-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.898073661-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.7","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952","title":"EPIC: Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)","description":"# Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)

## Goal
Make NTM feel calm, obvious, and premium — like a best-in-class product — while staying true to terminal constraints.

This is a **polish program epic**: it coordinates UX improvements across CLI + TUI, emphasizing:
- Clear information hierarchy (less visual noise)
- Predictable navigation (stable keymaps + help overlays)
- Actionable copy (every error tells you what to do next)
- Smoothness (minimal flicker/jitter, fast refresh, responsive interactions)
- Accessibility (reduced motion, icon fallbacks, contrast)

## Target User Journeys
1. **First run**: install → `ntm deps` → `ntm tutorial` → `ntm spawn` → `ntm palette` → `ntm dashboard`
2. **Daily loop**: attach → palette → send → dashboard → copy/save → detach
3. **Remote over SSH**: sessions persist, minimal surprises, no brittle quoting, clear "where am I" context

## Scope
- CLI: copywriting, error remediation, progress affordances
- Palette: search + target selection clarity + recents/favorites
- Dashboard: stable layout + help/legend + better empty/loading/error states
- Theme/accessibility: reduced motion + safe icon fallbacks + contrast
- Performance: refresh budgets, caching, graceful degradation
- Docs alignment (update existing docs; avoid new doc sprawl)

## Out of Scope (tracked elsewhere)
- New major product surfaces (e.g., VS Code extension) → see `ntm-97c5`
- New data sources/panels already covered under dashboard program → see `ntm-jpp1`
- Large config discovery work → see `ntm-bke.1`

## Related / Dependencies (foundations)
- Dashboard foundation: `ntm-jpp1`, `ntm-im5l`, `ntm-p99w`, `ntm-amou`
- Width + layout primitives: `ntm-ci35`
- Refresh cadence: `ntm-0c8q`
- Theme system: `ntm-ed5`, `ntm-ube`
- Context/session inference: `ntm-4ji`, `ntm-bke.1`","design":"## Design Principles (Stripe-level in terminal)
- **Clarity over cleverness**: effects exist to support hierarchy, not compete with content.
- **Predictability**: stable keymaps; no surprise mode switches; consistent back/escape behavior.
- **Actionability**: errors include cause + fix + suggested next command.
- **Continuity**: preserve scroll position / selection where possible; avoid jitter.
- **Graceful fallback**: icons, colors, widths, and animations degrade cleanly.
---

## How to Use This Epic

This epic is intentionally structured to keep UX work **actionable** and **non-duplicative**:
- Foundations already tracked elsewhere (dashboard, theme, config discovery).
- This epic focuses on the “last mile” that makes the product feel premium.

### Recommended Order
1. **Shared UX primitives** (`ntm-952.1.*`) → makes later work consistent.
2. **CLI remediation + session inference** (`ntm-952.2.*`) → improves day-to-day flows immediately.
3. **Palette + dashboard overlays + stability** (`ntm-952.3.*`, `ntm-952.4.*`) → the premium feel.
4. **A11y + perf** (`ntm-952.5.*`, `ntm-952.6.*`) → smooth + safe defaults.
5. **Docs alignment** (`ntm-952.7.*`) at the end of each sub-track.

### Success Heuristics
- If something fails, users know exactly what to do next.
- Key bindings are discoverable without reading docs.
- The UI is stable under refresh: selection doesn’t jump, panes don’t “wiggle”.
- Reduced motion + icon fallbacks make the UI usable everywhere.","acceptance_criteria":"- A consistent help/legend affordance exists in palette + dashboard.
- CLI errors are standardized and always include a remediation suggestion.
- Reduced-motion mode exists and disables pulsing/shimmer animations.
- Dashboard/palette interactions feel stable (minimal flicker/jump under refresh).
- Docs reflect the actual UX (keys, tiers, environment variables).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.798667661-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-jpp1","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ed5","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-bke.1","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ci35","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-0c8q","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ji","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952","depends_on_id":"ntm-ube","type":"related","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: UX Design System & Consistency","description":"Focus: consistency and “calm premium” feel across all TUIs.

Deliverables:
- Shared layout primitives and style tokens (spacing, borders, typography)
- Shared interaction patterns (help overlay, key hints, empty/loading states)
- A small set of conventions used everywhere (no per-screen one-offs)

Non-goals:
- Rewriting all screens at once
- Adding new major product features","design":"## Why this matters
A “Stripe-level” feel in terminal comes from **consistency** more than effects. If every screen invents its own spacing, borders, and help hints, the product feels noisy and amateur.

## Guidance
- Prefer shared helpers over one-off lipgloss styles.
- Design for **width tiers** and **icon sets** from day 1.
- Keep overlays minimal: show the top 10% of keys that drive 90% of usage.

## Downstream impact
Palette/dashboard polish tasks should reuse these primitives rather than re-implementing per-screen versions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.830598777-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1.1","title":"TUI: Shared empty/loading/error state components","description":"Introduce reusable UI components for empty/loading/error states so every panel/screen communicates status and next action consistently.","acceptance_criteria":"- Dashboard and palette use shared states\n- Includes retry/refresh affordance where applicable\n- Respects icon + width fallbacks","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.899229794-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.899229794-06:00","labels":["ux","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1.2","title":"TUI: Shared HelpOverlay + KeyHint patterns","description":"Implement a shared HelpOverlay (e.g., '?' or F1) and KeyHint rendering so palette/dashboard/tutorial all expose discoverable shortcuts with consistent visuals.","acceptance_criteria":"- Palette + dashboard expose the same help affordance\n- Key hints are width-aware and non-jittery\n- Escape/back behavior is documented and consistent","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.948718625-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.948718625-06:00","labels":["ux","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1.3","title":"TUI: Style tokens for spacing/borders/typography","description":"Centralize spacing, border, and text-style conventions into a small set of tokens/helpers to avoid per-screen drift and misalignment.","acceptance_criteria":"- Core style helpers exist and are used in new code\n- Common gutter widths are consistent across screens\n- No new one-off lipgloss styles without a clear reason","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.996387915-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:07.996387915-06:00","labels":["ux","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1.4","title":"TUI: Regression snapshots for overlays and empty states","description":"Add targeted snapshot/layout tests for the shared overlays and empty/loading/error states to prevent subtle UX regressions across width tiers.","acceptance_criteria":"- Tests cover narrow + split + wide tiers\n- Snapshots include help overlay + empty states\n- CI catches accidental layout drift","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:08.046387947-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:08.046387947-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.2","title":"SUB-EPIC: CLI Copy & Error Remediation","description":"Focus: CLI output that’s clear, actionable, and consistent.

Deliverables:
- Standard error format (cause → fix → next command)
- Better session selection/resolution when args omitted
- Consistent “what next” guidance after major commands","design":"## Error copy contract
Every error should include:
1) **What failed** (command + target)
2) **Why** (root cause in plain language)
3) **What to do next** (one concrete command)

## Session resolution contract
When a session argument is missing:
- Prefer deterministic inference (cwd → project/session mapping).
- If ambiguous, show an interactive chooser.
- Always echo the chosen session explicitly.

## Avoid
- One-off flags per command.
- Huge stack traces by default.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.862410571-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.2.1","title":"CLI: Standardize error format + remediation hints","description":"Make all CLI errors follow a consistent structure: what failed, why, and the fastest next command to fix it.","acceptance_criteria":"- Errors include cause + suggested fix\n- Output remains readable in both color and no-color modes\n- Exit codes remain correct","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacStone","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.362365323-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T13:19:02.863364677-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-12T13:19:02.863364677-06:00","labels":["ux","cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.2.2","title":"CLI: Session inference + interactive chooser when omitted","description":"When the session argument is omitted, infer intelligently (cwd/project) and fall back to an interactive chooser instead of hard-failing.","acceptance_criteria":"- Uses a single, shared session-resolution algorithm across commands\n- If multiple candidates exist, presents a chooser with clear defaults\n- Clearly explains which session was selected and why","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.41121191-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.41121191-06:00","labels":["ux","cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ji","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-bke.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.2.3","title":"CLI: Add 'What next' success footers for major commands","description":"After successful commands like spawn/quick/attach, print a short next-step footer that nudges users into the intended workflow.","acceptance_criteria":"- Success output includes 1-3 next commands (dashboard/palette/send)\n- Messaging is concise and skimmable\n- Can be disabled in robot/terse mode","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.507544927-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.507544927-06:00","labels":["ux","cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.2.4","title":"CLI: Standard progress patterns for long operations","description":"Unify progress messaging (steps/spinner) for long operations (quick, upgrade, spawn) so users trust the tool and understand what's happening.","acceptance_criteria":"- Long ops show step-oriented progress\n- Failures show which step failed and suggested recovery\n- No noisy logs by default","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.556823697-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:31.556823697-06:00","labels":["ux","cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.3","title":"SUB-EPIC: Command Palette Premium","description":"Focus: command palette that feels fast, obvious, and delightful.

Deliverables:
- Recents/favorites/pinning
- Crystal-clear target selection (who will receive this?)
- Preview that reduces misfires (metadata, warnings)
- Help overlay and consistent navigation","design":"## Palette = “fast path”
The palette should be the fastest way to:
- pick a vetted prompt
- choose recipients confidently
- send with minimal risk of misfire

## Premium feel comes from
- Recents/favorites (reduces re-searching)
- Clear recipient preview (reduces uncertainty)
- Stable filtering (no jumpy list)
- Help overlay (no docs required)

## Persistence
Prefer using existing config mechanisms (global + project), not inventing new storage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.793248034-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.893976239-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.3.1","title":"Palette: Recents, favorites, and pinning","description":"Add recents/favorites/pinning to make the palette feel like a power tool (less re-searching, fewer repeat mistakes).","acceptance_criteria":"- Recents list updates automatically\n- Favorites/pins are persisted\n- Works for both global and project palettes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.066500362-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.066500362-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","palette"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-bke.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.3.2","title":"Palette: Target selector clarity (counts + sample panes)","description":"Make the target selector answer “who will receive this?” at a glance: counts, representative pane names, and clear defaults.","acceptance_criteria":"- Target selector shows recipient count\n- Defaults are predictable and visible\n- Works across width tiers without wrapping","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.145211478-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.145211478-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","palette"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-ci35","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.3.3","title":"Palette: Search UX polish (highlighting + stable sort)","description":"Improve search feel: highlight match segments, maintain stable ordering, and avoid jumpy list behavior while typing.","acceptance_criteria":"- Match text is highlighted (with fallbacks)\n- Sorting is stable between keystrokes\n- No flicker/jitter when filtering","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownStone","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.222361503-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T13:16:48.053815664-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-12T13:16:48.053815664-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","palette"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.3.4","title":"Palette: Preview metadata + safety nudges + help overlay","description":"Upgrade the preview pane to reduce misfires: show prompt metadata (length/targets), optional warning badges, and a consistent help overlay.","acceptance_criteria":"- Preview shows target summary + prompt metadata\n- Optional lightweight warning badges (non-blocking)\n- Help overlay uses shared component","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.275478496-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:36:55.275478496-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","palette"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.4","title":"SUB-EPIC: Dashboard Premium","description":"Focus: dashboard interactions, information hierarchy, and stability under refresh.

Deliverables:
- Help/legend overlay and consistent keymap
- Better empty/loading/error states with retry affordances
- Reduced layout jitter/flicker
- Quick actions that match the real workflow","design":"## Dashboard success criteria
The dashboard should answer, at a glance:
- what’s running
- what’s broken
- what needs attention now

## Stability matters
A “premium” dashboard does not flicker or jump on refresh. Prioritize:
- stable selection/focus
- stable panel heights
- budgeted refresh/capture

## Help overlay
A lightweight '?' overlay is the single best discoverability upgrade for this surface.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.819145086-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.925212844-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.4.1","title":"Dashboard: Help/legend overlay + consistent keymap","description":"Add an in-dashboard help/legend overlay (e.g., '?') showing key bindings, icon legend, and navigation model.","acceptance_criteria":"- Help overlay is discoverable and non-intrusive\n- Uses shared HelpOverlay/KeyHint components\n- Keymap matches palette where reasonable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.343221856-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.343221856-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","dashboard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-im5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.4.2","title":"Dashboard: Panel empty/loading/error states + retry","description":"Ensure every dashboard panel has consistent empty/loading/error states with a clear retry/refresh affordance.","acceptance_criteria":"- Panels never render blank/ambiguous states\n- Retry/refresh action exists where applicable\n- Uses shared empty/loading/error components","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.443283929-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.443283929-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","dashboard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-p99w","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

+{"id":"ntm-952.4.4","title":"Dashboard: Quick actions bar (palette/send/copy/zoom)","description":"Add a small quick-actions area to match real workflows (open palette, send prompt to selected, copy output, zoom).","acceptance_criteria":"- Actions are keyboard-accessible\n- Layout is width-aware\n- Does not clutter narrow mode","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.637372088-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.637372088-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","dashboard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-im5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.4.5","title":"Dashboard: Header/statusline context polish","description":"Polish the dashboard header/statusline to answer “where am I?” (session, remote/local, last refresh) and reduce ambiguity.","acceptance_criteria":"- Header includes session + last refresh signal\n- Remote/local context is visible when relevant\n- Copy is concise and non-noisy","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.713792305-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:22.713792305-06:00","labels":["ux","tui","dashboard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-4ji","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.5","title":"SUB-EPIC: Accessibility & Terminal Compatibility","description":"Focus: accessibility and terminal compatibility.

Deliverables:
- Reduced-motion mode (no shimmer/pulse)
- Safer icon and width fallbacks (no broken gutters)
- Better contrast defaults and an escape hatch for low-color terminals","design":"## Terminal constraints
We can’t rely on:
- Nerd Fonts
- good color support
- wide panes
- low-latency refresh

So accessibility is largely about **fallbacks** and **stability**.

## Reduced motion
Make it easy to disable shimmer/pulse (including for CI snapshots).

## No-color
Honor NO_COLOR and avoid relying on color alone to convey meaning.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.955632272-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.5.1","title":"A11y: Reduced motion mode","description":"Add a reduced-motion mode that disables shimmer/pulse animations and prefers stable rendering for sensitive users and low-quality terminals.","acceptance_criteria":"- Env/config toggle exists (e.g., NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1)\n- Pulse/shimmer animations are disabled\n- Layout remains stable without animation artifacts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.184262642-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T13:17:29.879823589-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-12T13:17:29.879823589-06:00","labels":["ux","a11y","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-ube","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.5.2","title":"A11y: No-color / plain-output mode","description":"Support no-color/plain output for users who need maximum legibility or are piping output (honor NO_COLOR and provide NTM overrides).","acceptance_criteria":"- NO_COLOR is respected\n- CLI outputs remain clear without ANSI\n- TUIs avoid unreadable low-contrast combinations","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.259194282-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.259194282-06:00","labels":["ux","a11y","cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.5.3","title":"A11y: Icon fallback audit + stable widths","description":"Audit icon usage across TUIs to ensure nerd/unicode/ascii fallbacks never break gutters, alignment, or width calculations.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every icon has a stable-width fallback\n- Narrow terminals don't drift/misalign\n- Rendering remains deterministic across icon sets","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.310826122-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.310826122-06:00","labels":["ux","a11y","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-ci35","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.5.4","title":"A11y: Contrast audit + guard rails","description":"Audit theme contrast and add guard rails for low-color terminals (better defaults + an escape hatch).","acceptance_criteria":"- Contrast improves in critical UI areas (selection, errors, warnings)\n- Themes remain legible across light/dark backgrounds\n- Documented escape hatch for low-color terminals","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.384648491-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:37:43.384648491-06:00","labels":["ux","a11y","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-ube","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.6","title":"SUB-EPIC: Performance & Responsiveness","description":"Focus: responsiveness and reliability.

Deliverables:
- Refresh budgets and caching (avoid expensive tmux capture loops)
- Graceful degradation when tmux data is missing/slow
- Debug affordances for diagnosing “why is this slow?”","design":"## Performance philosophy
Do less work by default:
- cache tmux captures
- coalesce refreshes
- cap line budgets

## Measure
When possible, surface timing/latency in an opt-in debug view. It’s hard to polish what you can’t observe.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:40:48.986947614-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.6.1","title":"Perf: Refresh budgets + per-panel caching","description":"Introduce refresh budgets and caching so dashboards/palettes stay responsive even with many panes (avoid expensive tmux capture storms).","acceptance_criteria":"- Configurable refresh intervals exist\n- Expensive captures are cached/coalesced\n- Large sessions remain responsive","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.385608071-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.385608071-06:00","labels":["ux","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-0c8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.6.2","title":"Perf: Async fetch cancellation + coalescing","description":"Prevent wasted work by canceling or coalescing in-flight fetches when users switch panes/sessions or trigger refresh repeatedly.","acceptance_criteria":"- In-flight fetches can be canceled\n- Rapid refresh inputs are coalesced\n- UI never shows stale data as 'fresh'","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.46302027-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.46302027-06:00","labels":["ux","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.6.3","title":"Perf: Tmux capture optimization helpers","description":"Optimize tmux output capture usage (smaller ranges, incremental capture, shared helpers) to reduce latency and CPU.","acceptance_criteria":"- Capture helpers accept line budgets\n- Capture avoids unnecessary full-pane reads\n- Callers migrate to the optimized helpers","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.517764925-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.517764925-06:00","labels":["ux","perf","tmux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.6.4","title":"Perf: Graceful degradation + diagnostics","description":"When tmux data is missing/slow, degrade gracefully (clear UI states) and provide optional diagnostics for troubleshooting.","acceptance_criteria":"- UI shows explicit degraded states (not blank)\n- Errors include actionable hints\n- Optional debug view/logging exists for performance triage","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.570968996-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:38:01.570968996-06:00","labels":["ux","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

 {"id":"ntm-97c5","title":"EPIC: IDE Integration (VS Code)","description":"# IDE Integration (VS Code Extension)

## Background & Motivation

Most developers work primarily in VS Code. IDE integration reduces context switching and enables unique value that terminal-only can't provide.

## CRITICAL: Focus on Unique IDE Value

The terminal TUI is already excellent. Don't rebuild it in VS Code.

**Unique IDE value:**
1. **Editor context injection**: Send selected code/current file to agents
2. **Quick access**: Status bar for at-a-glance info
3. **File decorations**: Show lock status in explorer

**NOT prioritized:**
- Full dashboard webview (duplicates TUI)
- Complex session management (use terminal)

## What the Extension Provides

### 1. Status Bar (High Value, Low Effort)
```
[NTM: myproject ● 4 agents]
```
- Click to open command palette
- Shows session name, agent count
- Color indicates status (green=ok, yellow=warning)

### 2. Commands (High Value, Medium Effort)
```
NTM: Send Selection to Agent
NTM: Send Current File to Agent
NTM: Spawn Agents for Project
NTM: Show Session Status
NTM: Open Terminal (attach to session)
```

### 3. Context Injection (Killer Feature)
```typescript
// When user invokes "Send Selection"
const selection = editor.selection;
const text = editor.document.getText(selection);
const filePath = editor.document.fileName;

// Construct context-aware prompt
const prompt = `
File: ${filePath}
Selected code:
\`\`\`
${text}
\`\`\`

[User's instruction here]
`;

// Send via ntm send
await ntm.send(session, target, prompt);
```

### 4. File Decorations (Medium Value, Medium Effort)
Show lock status in file explorer:
- 🔒 icon for locked files
- Tooltip: "Locked by GreenCastle (cc_1)"
- Requires Agent Mail integration

### 5. Simple Session View (Optional)
Activity bar panel with:
- List of sessions
- Agent count per session
- Quick actions (attach, kill)

NOT a full dashboard - just navigation.

## What We're NOT Building

- ❌ Full webview dashboard (use terminal TUI)
- ❌ Inline agent chat (complex, scope creep)
- ❌ Custom editor for prompts (overkill)

## Technical Approach

### CLI Wrapper
```typescript
class NtmClient {
    async getStatus(): Promise<RobotStatus> {
        const result = await exec('ntm --robot-status');
        return JSON.parse(result.stdout);
    }
    
    async send(session: string, target: string, prompt: string): Promise<void> {
        await exec(`ntm send ${session} ${target} ${quote(prompt)}`);
    }
}
```

### Extension Activation
Activate when:
- Workspace contains .ntm/ directory
- User runs NTM command
- NTM status bar clicked

## Priority Order

1. **P2**: Status bar + basic commands (NTM: Spawn, Send)
2. **P2**: Context injection (Send Selection)
3. **P3**: File decorations (requires Agent Mail)
4. **P3**: Session tree view
5. **P4**: Webview (if ever)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Status bar shows current session
- [ ] Commands in palette for common actions
- [ ] "Send Selection to Agent" works with context
- [ ] Extension published to marketplace
- [ ] Minimal dependencies, fast activation
- [ ] Degrades gracefully if ntm not installed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:28:29.775574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:37:02.457909-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-k7o","title":"Integrate --ssh flag with all session commands","description":"Add --ssh support to spawn, list, attach, send, status, kill.

## What This Task Does

After the SSH wrapper (ntm-5zs) is complete, integrate --ssh flag
with all commands that operate on sessions.

## Commands to Update

- ntm spawn --ssh host: Create session on remote
- ntm list --ssh host: List remote sessions
- ntm attach --ssh host: Attach to remote session
- ntm send --ssh host: Send prompt to remote agents
- ntm status --ssh host: Get remote session status
- ntm kill --ssh host: Kill remote session

## Implementation Pattern

For each command:
1. Add --ssh flag (string, optional)
2. Pass to TmuxClient constructor
3. All tmux operations use client abstraction

func runSpawn(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    sshHost, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("ssh")
    client := tmux.NewClient(sshHost)
    
    // All operations use client
    if err := client.CreateSession(session); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    ...
}

## Session Host Persistence

When spawning remotely, store host in tmux environment:
client.SetEnvironment(session, "NTM_REMOTE_HOST", sshHost)

When operating on existing session without --ssh:
1. Get session's NTM_REMOTE_HOST
2. If set, use it
3. If not, assume local

## Error Handling

- Connection timeout: Clear message, suggest checking connectivity
- Auth failure: Suggest ssh-agent or key path
- Remote tmux not found: Suggest installing tmux on remote

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --ssh works on spawn
- [ ] --ssh works on list  
- [ ] --ssh works on attach
- [ ] --ssh works on send
- [ ] --ssh works on status
- [ ] --ssh works on kill
- [ ] Remote host auto-detected for existing sessions
